Both gifts and rewards can involve money or other things of even more value. And both gifts and rewards can be personalized. But gifts can be more powerful than rewards for a number of reasons. First, gifts can be proactive. They can come before you make a request. While rewards are given only after a person does something. Rewards are conditional. I'll only do X if you first do Y. Otherwise I won't. Gifts are unconditional. Next, rewards can suggest a lower level of trust indicating that only once an action is taken will the other person release the reward. Gifts on the other hand, as we've said, are unconditional in that they aren't contingent on anything from the other person. Gifts are given freely and open. Gifts typically begin and exchange, whereas rewards often end one. Because certain conditions have been met. For all these reasons, gifts provide certain advantages that rewards simply don't.
